label: CircleCI
description: Trigger and monitor CircleCI pipelines, workflows, and jobs from Anton.

form.title: Connect CircleCI
form.subtitle: Paste your CircleCI personal API token to give Anton access to your pipelines.

methods.label: Personal API Token
methods.description: Generate a personal API token from CircleCI User Settings and paste it here. Quickest setup — no OAuth app required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a CircleCI Personal API Token

1. Log in to [CircleCI](https://app.circleci.com/).
2. Click your avatar in the bottom-left corner and select **User Settings**.
3. In the left sidebar, click **Personal API Tokens**.
4. Click **Create New Token**.
5. Enter a name like `Anton` and click **Add API Token**.
6. Copy the token shown — it is displayed **only once**.
7. Paste it into the **API Token** field below.

## Notes

- The token inherits your personal permissions — it can access any project you have access to.
- If you lose the token, delete it in **User Settings → Personal API Tokens** and generate a new one.
- For server (self-hosted) installations, use your own CircleCI server URL in the **API Base URL** field.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.placeholder: Enter your CircleCI personal API token
fields.description: Found in CircleCI → User Settings → Personal API Tokens. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: api_base_url
fields.label: API Base URL
fields.description: Leave as default unless you are on a self-hosted CircleCI Server installation.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Connect via OAuth 2.0 using your own CircleCI OAuth app credentials. More setup required — recommended only for org-wide or automated deployments.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 with a CircleCI OAuth App

1. Go to [CircleCI Developer Hub](https://circleci.com/developer/) and log in.
2. Navigate to **Organization Settings → OAuth Apps** (or contact CircleCI support to register an OAuth app if the option is not visible).
3. Create a new OAuth application:
   - Set the **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton handles the local redirect).
   - Note the generated **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
4. Paste those values into the fields below.
5. Click **Submit** — a browser window will open for you to authorize Anton.

## Notes

- OAuth app registration may require a CircleCI plan that supports it. Check your org settings.
- If OAuth setup is not available for your account, use the **Personal API Token** method instead.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.placeholder: Your CircleCI OAuth app client ID
fields.description: From CircleCI Organization Settings → OAuth Apps.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.placeholder: Your CircleCI OAuth app client secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID from the same OAuth app registration.
